title: Portfolio Case Studies and Technical Decision Narratives · Career Evidence Case: Decision Trace
summary: A project page celebrates features and tools but omits the user need, personal decisions, alternatives, failures, verification, accessibility, security, impact limits, and maintenance state. Predict the hiring decision, evidence quality, affected people, uncertainty, and ethical boundary before reviewing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the decision trace increment in evidence-linked project case-study set. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, c…
objectives: Present important alternatives, tradeoffs, revision triggers, and the learner-owned technical decisions rather than a tool inventory.
Explain the affected people, decision, evidence, constraints, prior alternative, and personal authority without inventing user research.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Predict Decision Trace · Theory Decision Trace
steps.instruction:
theory case — A polished project story lists features and tools but invents user impact, hides failures, and links no accessibility, security, changed-case, or operations evidence. The theory variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Decision Trace contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Decision Trace · Theory Decision Trace.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for present important alternatives, tradeoffs, revision triggers, and the learner-owned technical decisions rather than a tool inventory. For Predict Decision Trace · Theory Decision Trace, keep Decision Trace connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A polished project story lists features and tools but invents user impact, hides failures, and links no accessibility, security, changed-case, or operations evidence. The theory variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Decision Trace condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: FTC Job Scam Consumer Alert — Controls recruiter identity verification, premature sensitive-data requests, payment and equipment-check fraud, urgency signals, and independent employer-channel confirmation.

hints: For Predict Decision Trace · Theory Decision Trace, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Decision Trace precondition, or invariant that must hold.

For Predict Decision Trace · Theory Decision Trace, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: Naming a modern stack demonstrates architectural judgment.

options.text: Present important alternatives, tradeoffs, revision triggers, and the learner-owned technical decisions rather than a tool inventory. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.
Naming a modern stack demonstrates architectural judgment. That claim fails the predict decision trace evidence boundary for Decision Trace.

For predict decision trace, apply Decision Trace without its stated preconditions, omit the evidence-linked project case-study set trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Decision Trace for evidence-linked project case-study set.

content.text: Present important alternatives, tradeoffs, revision triggers, and the learner-owned technical decisions rather than a tool inventory. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, changed candidate, rejected failure, causal repair, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer limit; then calibrate the application or career decision to that exact evidence. Apply that move specifically to Decision Trace while producing evidence-linked project case-study set.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A polished project story lists features and tools but invents user impact, hides failures, and links no accessibility, security, changed-case, or operations evidence. The worked example variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Present important alternatives, tradeoffs, revision triggers, and the learner-owned technical decisions rather than a tool inventory. A defensible solution applies Decision Trace, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to evidence-linked project case-study set.

content.code:
# Worked focus: career-decision-trace — Present important alternatives, tradeoffs, revision triggers, and the learner-owned technical decisions rather than a tool inventory.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-candidate-3
focus: career-decision-trace
decision-evidence: Present important alternatives, tradeoffs, revision triggers, and the learner-owned technical decisions rather than a tool inventory.
target-role: accessible software quality engineer
jurisdiction: example location requiring current official review
audience: technical reviewer with ten minutes and keyboard-only access
claim: one bounded role behavior is supported by a revision-linked changed case
evidence: repository case study, exact test result, contribution note, and limitation
process-state: investigate before applying
accessibility: structured text, reflow, visible focus, plain-language status, and equivalent format
privacy-consent: publish no sensitive person or third-party data; contact only with explicit consent
decision: proceed only after identity, role, evidence, access, and risk checks pass
verify: inspect links, extraction order, revision, changed case, disclosure, and source date
failure: reject invented impact, hidden assistance, exposed data, or unverified recruiter identity
repair: correct the first unsupported claim and add a changed-candidate regression
owner: learner controls every external action
transfer-boundary: manual learner-approved profile, application, conversation, interview, and offer decisions
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Naming a modern stack demonstrates architectural judgment. This fails the Decision Trace contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: FTC Job Scam Consumer Alert (Published 2025-07-10; reviewed 2026-07-15) — Controls recruiter identity verification, premature sensitive-data requests, payment and equipment-check fraud, urgency signals, and independent employer-channel confirmation.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Present important alternatives, tradeoffs, revision triggers, and the learner-owned technical decisions rather than a tool inventory.
